items.title: Costa Rica's President Cuts Off Diplomatic Ties With Cuban Regime
items.deck: Costa Rica's President Cuts Off Diplomatic Ties With Cuban Regime Authored by Kimberlyh Hayek via The Epoch Times (emphasis ours), Costa Rica’s President Rodrigo Chaves revealed Wednesday that his government has ceased recognizing the legitimacy of Cuba’s communist regime and ordered the Cuban embassy in San José to close.

items.title: RUSSELL FINDLAY: Voting for Reform only risks handing keys to Bute House back to Swinney
items.deck: If Nigel Farage's Reform UK can achieve one thing at the Holyrood election, it will be this: they will hand John Swinney the keys to Bute House.

items.title: Ex-FBI agents who worked on Trump 2020 election probe sue over firings
items.deck: Two former FBI agents who helped investigate President Trump's efforts to overturn the 2020 presidential election results sued the federal government, alleging they were wrongfully terminated.

items.title: Gamers Rebel Against Nvidia’s DLSS 5 ‘AI Slop Filter’
items.deck: Nvidia’s DLSS 5 graphics upgrade sparked backlash, memes and mockery, with gamers rejecting the proposed “AI slop” filter.

items.title: Taylor Frankie Paul seen attacking ex boyfriend with chair in newly released video
items.deck: A new video released on Thursday shows Taylor Frankie Paul attacking her ex-boyfriend, Dakota Mortensen, in front of her daughter three years ago.

items.title: Investigators taking closer look at Nancy Guthrie’s security gate
items.deck: The new theory comes as the FBI returns to the neighborhood with new questions for residents there.

items.title: How an act of civil disobedience in 1978 Denver helped propel disability rights
items.deck: On July 5-6, 1978, on a busy downtown Denver street, 19 people in wheelchairs blocked public buses--which didn't have wheelchair lifts--to demand access to public transit.

items.title: Ex-Republican Rep. Kevin Kiley leaves door open to caucusing with Dems
items.deck: Rep. Kevin Kiley (I-Calif.) ditched the GOP earlier this month.

items.title: Norway crown princess to address Epstein links as son's rape trial closes
items.deck: Mette-Marit gives a much-anticipated interview, due to be broadcast on Friday.

items.title: New FIFA rule says women's teams must have a woman on leadership staff
items.deck: FIFA’s council passed a new rule Thursday aimed at bolstering the role of women in leadership, changing its participation requirements for all nations ahead of the 2027 women’s World Cup.
